# The Mountain — Groove Box

> A browser-based 6-channel generative music mixer with per-channel Lead and Bach modes. Runs entirely in the browser — no server, no account, no cost. Built with Web Audio API, React 19, Vite, and TypeScript.

## What it does

Six instruments — bass, pluck, pad, drums, cello, flute — each running a generative loop over a repeating Am→G→C→F 4-bar progression. Every channel has three behavioral modes:

- **Normal** — steady rhythmic pattern
- **Lead ⚡** — takes the melodic foreground, improvises above the bed
- **Bach `[CPU]`** — plays a contrapuntal two-voice (left hand / right hand) arrangement using Am7/G7/Cmaj7/Fmaj7 voicings. Bar 3 triggers a Fibonacci-indexed variant.

Channels can stack freely. The whole thing runs offline.

## Live

https://yellowhapax.github.io/MusicBox/

## Source

https://github.com/YellowHapax/MusicBox

## License

MIT. Free to use, study, remix, deploy.

## Stack

- Web Audio API (no libraries)
- React 19 + TypeScript
- Vite 6 + Tailwind v4
- motion/react, lucide-react

## Author

YellowHapax — https://github.com/YellowHapax
